content.text: Circuit Tracks features two independent polyphonic synthesizer tracks. Each synth track can play up to 6 notes simultaneously and comes loaded with 128 factory patches.

Press the Synth 1 or Synth 2 button to select that track. The button will light up to indicate it's selected. Once selected, the pads become a chromatic keyboard and the macro knobs control that synth's parameters.
Double-tap a synth track button to enter the patch browser. Use the pads to preview patches, or turn Macro 1 to scroll through the list. Press the synth button again to confirm your selection.
Patches are organized into banks. The RGB pad colors indicate the bank and patch location as you browse.
The eight macro knobs provide hands-on control of the most important synth parameters. Each patch has unique macro assignments designed by the sound designer.

content.caption: Eight macro knobs for sound design

content.text: Hold Shift while turning a macro knob for fine adjustment.
Filter parameters are controlled via the macro knobs. Macro 4 (FILTER) controls cutoff frequency, and Macro 6 (RES) controls resonance in most patches. These are labeled on the hardware panel.

items: Filter (Macro 4): Controls the cutoff frequency. Turn clockwise to brighten the sound.
Resonance (Macro 6): Adds emphasis at the cutoff frequency. High values create a whistling effect.

content.text: With a synth track selected, the pads become a chromatic keyboard. The layout is isomorphic - each row is a fourth above the previous.
The pads are velocity-sensitive. Hit harder for louder, brighter notes.
Use Novation's free Components software to dive deeper into synthesis. You can edit all synthesizer parameters, create custom patches, and manage your patch library.
